I recommend the local cuisine, and please, let me treat you tonight.

Would you like to join us? I recommend the local cuisine, and let us catch up properly.
Daniela, great talk today! A few of us are going to dinner, would you like to join us?
I would love to! Thank you for the invitation. Where is the venue?
A nice Brazilian place near the hotel. How about meeting in the lobby at seven thirty?
Perfect. And since it is Brazilian, let me recommend a few dishes, you should try the local cuisine.
Wonderful. And the first round is on me, I will treat everyone tonight.
That is very kind! Then we should make a toast, to a great conference.
Exactly. It will be nice to catch up properly, away from the conference rooms.
I would love that. I will see you at seven thirty in the lobby, then.
Would you like to join us for dinner?
I will have the fish, please. I recommend the local cuisine.
It was great to see you, let us catch up again soon.

Hi Daniela, it is Sarah, your English coach. Today, listen to one short tip about social English, the language you use after a conference, at dinner, when the work is over and you just want to connect. The most important thing is the invitation. To invite someone, you have three easy options. Would you like to grab dinner with us? How about dinner tonight? Or, do you want to join us for dinner? All three are warm and polite. Now, how do you answer? If you can go, say, I'd love to, or that sounds great. If you cannot, never just say no. Soften it. Say, I'd love to, but I have an early flight. The but plus a short reason makes it kind, not cold. And at the table, two phrases save you. To order, say, I'll have the fish, please. And to recommend, say, you should try the local cuisine, it is excellent. One more thing. At the end, do not say goodbye like a robot. Say, let's catch up again soon, or it was great to see you. That is real social English. Try it tonight. Talk soon.

Hi Daniela, it is Greg. Great to see you at the conference today, that talk on retail analytics was really good. Listen, a few of us from the international team are going to dinner tonight, and I wanted to invite you. Would you like to join us? We found a nice venue near the hotel, a Brazilian place actually, and they say the local cuisine there is excellent. We are meeting at seven thirty in the lobby. If you are free, just come down, no need to confirm. And honestly, the first round is on me, I'll treat everyone, so do not worry about the bill. It would be great to catch up properly, away from the conference rooms, and talk about something other than work for once. So, hope you can make it. If you cannot, no problem at all, but it would be lovely to see you there. See you tonight, I hope. Take care, Daniela.
